items.title: When Tragedy Goes Viral: What Social Media Exposure Means For Mental Health In Malaysia — Dr Lim Poh Khuen
items.summary: Mental health protection in the digital age is not solely an individual responsibility, but a broader public health concern. The post When Tragedy Goes Viral: What Social Media Exposure Means For Mental Health In Malaysia — Dr Lim Poh Khuen appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Government Backbenchers Tout Base MHIT To Mitigate Health Spending Cuts
items.summary: BBC chair Dr Zaliha Mustafa wants the Base MHIT to be launched quickly to mitigate Putrajaya's health spending cuts, so as to reduce dependence on public health care funded by the government. Base MHIT is meant to be offered by private insurance companies. The post Government Backbenchers Tout Base MHIT To Mitigate Health Spending Cuts appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: ‘Every Clinician Carries A Small Cemetery’: Finding Healing And Hope Through A-Shield — Dr Muhammad Yassin
items.summary: The A‑Shield framework sits comfortably within this philosophy: investigations focus on facts and processes, while peer support focuses on the person. The post ‘Every Clinician Carries A Small Cemetery’: Finding Healing And Hope Through A-Shield — Dr Muhammad Yassin appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Agriculture Ministry Braces For Potential Development Spending Cuts
items.summary: A senior KPKM official warns of potential cuts to agricultural development expenditure, such as irrigation systems, saying the money may be redirected to fund the country's mounting fuel subsidies. The post Agriculture Ministry Braces For Potential Development Spending Cuts appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Cut The Budget, Not The System: A Call For Health Care Stakeholders To Unite Now — Dr Raymond Choy
items.summary: Cost optimisation must never come at the expense of access or quality. Instead, it should drive smarter design, ensuring that care is delivered earlier, more efficiently, and in the most appropriate setting. The post Cut The Budget, Not The System: A Call For Health Care Stakeholders To Unite Now — Dr Raymond Choy appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Malaysia Needs A Health Service Commission Authorised With Manpower, Funding Powers — MMA
items.summary: Malaysia needs a Health Service Commission — with full independence over manpower and funding. Health care workforce planning cannot operate effectively within a one-size-fits-all civil service framework. The post Malaysia Needs A Health Service Commission Authorised With Manpower, Funding Powers — MMA appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Health Workers Need Clarity, Not ‘Strategic Directions’ — Clinician
items.summary: Health frontliners don't need more frameworks. We don't need more “strategic directions” or “ongoing engagements.” Many of us don't like fluff; we don't need unnecessary content packaged as reform. We need clarity, alignment, timelines and decisions. The post Health Workers Need Clarity, Not ‘Strategic Directions’ — Clinician appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Anwar: Only ‘Luxurious’ Scope Of Hospital Projects Targeted In Spending Cuts
items.summary: On health spending cuts, PM Anwar Ibrahim says only the "more luxurious" scope of hospital projects will be "reduced slightly". It's unclear if development expenditure is also targeted in the government's austerity drive, besides operational expenditure. The post Anwar: Only ‘Luxurious’ Scope Of Hospital Projects Targeted In Spending Cuts appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Health Service Commission May Not Necessarily Solve HR Problems — Dr KA
items.summary: A Health Service Commission (SPK) isn't necessarily the solution to HR problems in the health service because if it follows the Education Service Commission's model, the SPK won't have the power to create new positions or autonomy of in-service training. The post Health Service Commission May Not Necessarily Solve HR Problems — Dr KA appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Health Workers Brace For Death By A Thousand Cuts
items.summary: Pharmacists expect a reduction in drug dispensing supply and cuts to more effective medicines, ahead of plans to gut health spending by RM4.65 billion (10% of MOH’s budget). Doctors refuse to work for free if on-call claims are barred in allowance cuts. The post Health Workers Brace For Death By A Thousand Cuts appeared first on CodeBlue .
